description: You can smite creatures with the fire subtype. Once per day, you may attempt to smite a creature with the fire subtype. You add your Charisma modifier to your attack roll and deal 1 extra point of cold damage per character level.

name: Snowcasting
description: You add ice or snow to your spell's components to make them more powerful. If you add a handful of snow or ice as an additional material component, the spell gains the cold descriptor. If added to a spell with the cold descriptor, you increase the effective level by +1.

name: Snowflake Wardance
description: You have mastered the snowflake wardance, a mystical fighting style with slashing weapons. By expending one daily use of bardic music, you add your Charisma modifier to attack rolls with any slashing melee weapon you wield in one hand for a number of rounds equal to your ranks in Perform (dance).

name: Snowrunner
description: You are particularly adept at moving through snow and over ice. You can move through loose snow at full speed. You gain a +5 competence bonus on Balance checks to avoid slipping when you move across icy surfaces.

name: Storm Magic
description: You gain a boost in spellcasting power during storms. All spells you cast while you are affected by a storm (either natural or magical) manifest at +1 caster level.

name: Sugliin Mastery
description: You are a master at fighting with the massive sugliin. You can attack with a sugliin as if it were a normal weapon, making a single attack as a standard action or a full attack as a full-round action.
prerequisites: Base attack bonus +5, Exotic Weapon Proficiency (sugliin)

name: Winter's Champion
description: Your paladin spell list is enhanced. You may add the 1st-, 2nd-, 3rd-, and 4th-level spells granted by the Cold and Winter domains as paladin spells of the same levels.

name: Winter's Mount
description: Your special mount is native to the frostfell. Your special mount gains the cold subtype, immunity to cold, vulnerability to fire, and a +2 racial bonus to Constitution.

name: Ghost Attack
benefits: When you expend your psionic focus and score a critical hit against an incorporeal target, you ignore the 50% miss chance and can roll damage normally.

name: Gloom Razor
description: Advanced Shadow Hand tactics

name: Gnome Foe Killer
benefits: You gain a +2 bonus on weapon damage rolls against kobolds and goblinoids (goblins, hobgoblins, and bugbears).

name: Goad
benefits: As a standard action, you can goad an opponent into attacking you. Make a Bluff check opposed by the target's Sense Motive. If you succeed, the target can only attack you for 1 round.

name: Good Devotion
benefits: Once per day as an immediate action, you can surround yourself with an aura of good that grants you and each of your allies within 30 feet damage reduction that can be overcome only by evil-aligned weapons. The numeric value of this damage reduction is 1 + 1/five character levels you possess (maximum 5/evil at 20th level). In addition, your and your allies' natural and weapon attacks are good-aligned for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction. This effect lasts for 1 minute.
special: You can select this feat multiple times, gaining one additional daily use each time you take it.
If you have the ability to turn or rebuke undead, you gain one additional daily use of this feat for each daily turn or rebuke use you expend.

name: Good Karma
benefits: You can expend one luck reroll as an immediate action to redirect an attack made against an adjacent ally so that it is made against you instead. You must be within reach of the attacker (if a melee attack) or within range of the attack (if a ranged attack). The attack roll result remains the same, but it is against your AC, rather than that of your ally. If the redirected attack hits you, you take an extra 50% damage from it. You gain one luck reroll per day.

name: Great and Small

prerequisites.description: Wild shape class feature, ability to assume the form of a Large creature using wild shape

benefits: You can spend one daily use of your wild shape ability to either grow or shrink one size category. You cannot use this feat multiple times to grow or shrink more than one category beyond your normal size, nor does this effect stack with others that change your size. You can activate this feat only while in your normal form. This supernatural ability otherwise functions as the spells enlarge person and reduce person (caster level equals your character level).

name: Great Charisma
benefits: Your Charisma increases by 1 point.

name: Great Constitution
benefits: Your Constitution increases by 1 point.

name: Great Dexterity
benefits: Your Dexterity increases by 1 point.

name: Great Fortitude
description: +2 bonus on Fortitude saves.
benefits: You get a +2 bonus on all Fortitude saving throws.

name: Great Intelligence
benefits: Your Intelligence increases by 1 point.

name: Great Strength
benefits: Your Strength increases by 1 point.

name: Great Wisdom
benefits: Your Wisdom increases by 1 point.

name: Great Captain
description: If you are in command of a vessel, you can use the aid another action to assist all other persons on board who are performing the same action in the current round. Commanding the vessel in combat is a move action for you.

name: Greater Kiai Shout

prerequisites.description: Cha 13, Kiai Shout, base attack bonus +9

benefits: Your kiai shout can panic opponents for 2d6 rounds (Will save). Affects only opponents with fewer HD than you. Three times per day.

name: Greater Manyshot
benefits: As a standard action, you can fire two arrows at a single target or at separate targets within 30 feet of each other. As a full-round action, you can fire arrows at targets equal to your base attack bonus (max).

name: Greater Power Penetration
benefits: +8 total bonus on manifester level checks to overcome power resistance (replaces Power Penetration's +4 bonus).

name: Greater Power Specialization
benefits: +4 total bonus to damage with powers that deal hit point damage (replaces Power Specialization's +2 bonus).

name: Greater Psionic Endowment
benefits: When you expend your psionic focus, add +2 to the save DC of a power you manifest (replaces Psionic Endowment's +1).

name: Greater Psionic Fist
benefits: When you expend your psionic focus, your unarmed strike or natural weapon deals an extra 4d6 points of damage (replaces Psionic Fist's 2d6).

name: Greater Psionic Shot
benefits: When you expend your psionic focus, your ranged weapon attack deals an extra 4d6 points of damage (replaces Psionic Shot's 2d6).

name: Greater Psionic Weapon
benefits: When you expend your psionic focus, your melee weapon attack deals an extra 4d6 points of damage (replaces Psionic Weapon's 2d6).

name: Greater Resiliency

prerequisites.description: Damage reduction as a class feature or innate ability

benefits: Your damage reduction increases by 1.

name: Greater Two-Weapon Defense

prerequisites.description: Dex 19, Improved Two-Weapon Defense, Two-Weapon Defense, Two-Weapon Fighting, base attack bonus +11

benefits: Gain +3 shield bonus to AC when wielding two weapons (+6 when fighting defensively or using total defense).

name: Green Ear
benefits: You can affect plants with your bardic music ability as if they were creatures.

name: Group Inspiration
benefits: You can use your bardic music to inspire multiple groups simultaneously.

name: Guardian Spirit
benefits: Your watchful spirit allows you to reroll your initiative two times per day, as well as allowing you to reroll any saving throw once per day. These effects must be used immediately after the initial initiative check or saving throw is made.

name: Hamstring

prerequisites.description: Sneak attack ability, base attack bonus +4

benefits: Trade 2d6 sneak attack damage to reduce opponent's speed by half for 24 hours (or until healed).

name: Hardened Flesh
description: Zombies you create have tougher, more resilient flesh.
prerequisites: Corpsecrafter, ability to cast animate dead

name: Hawk's Vision
benefits: You can spend one use of your wild shape ability to gain a +8 bonus on Spot checks and cut range increment penalties in half for 1 hour.

name: Head Shot
benefits: Your successful sneak attack with a bludgeoning weapon for which you have selected Weapon Focus leaves your foe confused for 1 round. A successful Will save (DC 10 + the number of extra damage dice normally dealt by your sneak attack + your Dex modifier) negates this effect. Using this feat reduces your sneak attack damage by 5d6. You must be able to reach your foe's head to use this feat with a melee attack.

name: Healer's Luck
benefits: You can expend one luck reroll as a swift action to reroll the number of points of damage healed by a conjuration (healing) spell you have just cast on your current turn. You gain one luck reroll per day.

name: Healing Devotion
benefits: Once per day, you can gain fast healing 1, +1 for every five character levels you possess (maximum fast healing 5 at 20th level). This effect lasts for 1 minute. You can activate this feat as an immediate action or, if you have a daily use available, it automatically activates if you are reduced to 0 hit points or below (but not killed).
special: As a full-round action, you can transfer this ability to a willing recipient as a touch spell. Doing so counts as one daily use of the ability.
You can select this feat multiple times, gaining one additional daily use each time you take it.
If you have the ability to turn or rebuke undead, you gain one additional daily use of this feat for each daily turn or rebuke use you expend.

name: Healing Soul
description: You can invest essentia to heal yourself as a swift action.

name: Hear the Unseen
benefits: Before making a melee attack against a concealed or invisible opponent, you can make a Listen check to pinpoint the target's location. The Listen check DC is the same as for pinpointing an invisible creature (DC 20). If you succeed, you can make the attack without the standard miss chance.

name: Heart of the Nabassu
description: Your ancestry traces back to a place where the Abyss meets the Negative Energy Plane. You can absorb harmlessly a number of negative levels per day equal to the number of Abyssal heritor feats you possess.
source: Fiendish Codex I: Hordes of the Abyss

name: Heavy Lithoderms
benefits: Your natural armor bonus increases by 1.

name: Heighten Spell-Like Ability

prerequisites.description: Spell-like ability at caster level 6th or higher

benefits: Choose one of your spell-like abilities to use at a heightened level up to three times per day (or the ability's normal use limit, whichever is less). The spell-level equivalent of the heightened spell-like ability is two higher than its normal level (to a maximum of 9th level). Special: This feat can be taken multiple times.

name: Hold the Line
benefits: Make an attack of opportunity against a charging opponent who enters your threatened area. The attack happens before the charge is resolved.

name: Holy Potency

prerequisites.description: Base attack bonus +4, ability to spontaneously cast cure or inflict spells, ability to turn or rebuke undead

benefits: This feat allows you the use of two of the four following tactical maneuvers. The first two maneuvers are available only if you channel positive energy, while the latter two are available only if you channel negative energy. Balance of Life: To use this maneuver, you must first cast a cure spell, then attempt either a melee attack or a Strength-based check in the following round. You gain a bonus on that attack roll or check equal to the level of the cure spell cast. Conduit of Life: You must first attempt to turn undead, then make an attack with a cure spell against an undead creature in the following round. You gain a bonus on your damage roll for the spell equal to one-half the result of the initial turning check. Conduit of Death: You must first attempt to rebuke undead, then make an attack with an inflict spell against a living creature in the following round. Touch of Death: To use this maneuver, you must first cast an inflict spell, then attempt a melee attack against a living creature in the following round. If the attack hits, it deals extra damage equal to twice the level of the inflict spell cast.

name: Holy Strike
benefits: Your unarmed strikes are treated as good-aligned weapons, dealing +2d6 damage against evil creatures.

name: Holy Warrior

prerequisites.description: Ability to cast 4th-level spells, access to the War domain

benefits: As long as you have a 4th-level or higher War domain spell available to cast, you gain a bonus on your weapon damage rolls equal to the level of the highest-level War spell you have available to cast. As a secondary benefit, you gain a +1 competence bonus to your caster level when casting force spells.

name: Hostile Mind
benefits: You automatically deal 2d6 points of damage to any creature that successfully uses a telepathic attack or power on you. Only applies to psionic attacks.
special: Cannot take this feat if you have the ability to use powers.

name: Illumian Loremaster
benefits: Choose one Knowledge skill. You gain a +4 bonus on checks with that skill and can make checks with that skill untrained.

name: Imbued Healing

prerequisites.description: Ability to cast conjuration (healing) spells, access to one or more domains

benefits: Whenever you cast a 1st-level or higher conjuration (healing) spell, you not only heal your subject of hit point damage, but you also confer on it a carrier effect deriving from a domain to which you have access. Each carrier effect has a duration of 1 minute per level of the conjuration (healing) spell cast. Examples: Air (Electricity resistance 5), Evil (DR 3/good), Fire (Fire resistance 5), Good (DR 3/evil), Healing (1 temporary hp per level or HD), Protection (+2 sacred or profane bonus to AC when fighting defensively).
special: When you use a conjuration (healing) spell to deal damage to a target, you cannot confer one of these carrier effects on that target.
Different carrier effects can affect the same target concurrently, even if the domains are normally opposed.
If your conjuration (healing) spell affects multiple creatures, you can choose only one carrier effect per casting. All targets are subject to that same effect.

name: Impeding Attack
benefits: Your successful sneak attack imposes a –2 penalty on the target's Strength and Dexterity checks, as well as on any Strength- and Dexterity-based skill checks, for 10 rounds. Using this feat reduces your sneak attack damage by 3d6.

name: Imprint Stone
benefits: You can create power stones (psionic versions of scrolls).

name: Improved Buckler Defense
benefits: When attacking with an off-hand weapon, you retain your buckler's shield bonus to AC.

name: Improved Combat Casting
benefits: You don't incur attacks of opportunity for casting spells when threatened.

name: Improved Combat Expertise

prerequisites.description: Int 13, Combat Expertise, base attack bonus +6

benefits: When using Combat Expertise, the number you subtract can be any amount up to your base attack bonus (not just +5).

name: Improved Combat Reflexes
benefits: There is no limit to the number of attacks of opportunity you can make per round (normally limited by your Dex modifier).

name: Improved Diversion
benefits: You can use the Bluff skill to create a diversion and then hide as a move action (rather than two separate standard actions).

name: Improved Draconic Breath
benefits: The damage of your draconic breath weapon increases to 4d6, and you can use it twice per day.

name: Improved Familiar

prerequisites.description: Ability to acquire a new familiar, compatible alignment, sufficiently high arcane spellcaster level

benefits: When you choose a familiar, creatures such as Monstrous centipede (Small), Badger, Monstrous scorpion (Small), Viper (Medium), Monstrous spider (Small), Vargouille, and Mephits are also available. You can choose a familiar with an alignment up to one step away on each of the alignment axes.

name: Improved Favored Enemy

prerequisites.description: Favored enemy ability, base attack bonus +5

benefits: Deal +3 damage against your favored enemies. Stacks with other favored enemy bonuses.

name: Improved Flight
benefits: Your flight maneuverability improves by one step (clumsy to poor, poor to average, average to good, good to perfect).

name: Improved Initiative
description: +4 bonus on initiative checks.
benefits: You get a +4 bonus on initiative checks.

name: Improved Ki Strike
benefits: Your ki strike gains an additional +1 enhancement bonus, and your unarmed attacks are treated as having an alignment of your choice for overcoming damage reduction.

name: Improved Metamagic
benefits: Choose one metamagic feat. The spell slot penalty for that feat is reduced by 1 level (minimum +1).

name: Improved Mounted Archery

prerequisites.description: Ride 1 rank, Mounted Archery, Mounted Combat

benefits: Eliminate penalty when using ranged weapons while mount takes double move. Reduce running penalty from -4 to -2.

name: Improved Psicrystal
benefits: You can implant another personality fragment in your psicrystal, giving it one additional special ability.

name: Improved Rapid Shot

prerequisites.description: Manyshot, Point Blank Shot, Rapid Shot

benefits: When using Rapid Shot, ignore the -2 penalty on all ranged attack rolls.

name: Improved Skirmish
benefits: If you move at least 20 feet away from where you were at the start of your turn, your skirmish damage increases by 2d6 and your competence bonus to AC from skirmish improves by 2.

name: Improved Sneak Attack
benefits: Your sneak attack damage increases by +1d6.

name: Improved Spell Capacity
benefits: You can prepare and cast one additional spell per day of 10th level or higher. You must choose which level of spell you gain (10th-20th).

name: Improved Spell Resistance
benefits: Your spell resistance increases by 2.

name: Improved Stunning Fist
benefits: You add +2 to the DC of your stunning fist, and you can use it 5 additional times per day.

name: Improved Swimming
benefits: Your swim speed doubles. If you don't have a swim speed, you gain a swim speed equal to half your land speed.

name: Improved Toughness
benefits: Gain hit points equal to your current Hit Dice. Each time you gain a HD, you gain 1 additional hit point.

name: Improved Two-Weapon Defense

prerequisites.description: Dex 17, Two-Weapon Defense, Two-Weapon Fighting, base attack bonus +6

benefits: Gain +2 shield bonus to AC when wielding two weapons (+4 when fighting defensively or using total defense).

name: Improved Weapon Familiarity
benefits: Treat all exotic weapons associated with your race as martial weapons.

name: Improved Whirlwind Attack
benefits: As a standard action, you can make one melee attack for each opponent within reach.

name: Incarnum-Fortified Body
description: You can invest essentia to gain damage reduction.

name: Incarnum Spellshaping
description: You can invest essentia into a spell to increase its save DC.
prerequisites: Ability to cast spells, ability to invest essentia

name: Inhuman Reach
description: Your arms elongate, allowing you to touch the floor with your hands. You gain an additional 5 feet of reach. Your elongated arms also grant you a +2 bonus on Climb checks. However, you take a -1 penalty on all melee attack rolls.

name: Inhuman Vision
description: You possess the inhuman eyes of some strange creature. You gain a racial bonus on Spot checks equal to the number of aberrant feats you possess. The range of your darkvision improves by 5 feet for every aberrant feat that you possess. If you do not already have darkvision, you gain darkvision out to 5 feet for each aberrant feat you possess.

name: Innate Spell

prerequisites.description: Quicken Spell, Silent Spell, Still Spell

benefits: Choose any spell you can cast. You can now cast this spell at will as a spell-like ability once per round. One spell slot eight levels higher than the innate spell is permanently used to power it, and any XP cost for the innate spell is paid each time you use it. Special: You can choose this feat more than once.

name: Inquisitor
benefits: When you expend your psionic focus, you gain a +10 bonus on Sense Motive checks to oppose Bluff checks.

name: Insightful
benefits: An innate talent for magic grants you the following spell-like abilities as a 1st-level caster: 1/day—detect magic, detect secret doors, read magic.

name: Insightful Reflexes
benefits: You add your Intelligence modifier instead of your Dexterity modifier to Reflex saves.

name: Instantaneous Rage
benefits: You can rage even when it isn't your turn.

name: Intensify Spell
benefits: Variable, numeric effects of an intensified spell are increased by 100%. An intensified spell uses up a spell slot 8 levels higher.

name: Intimidating Rage
benefits: Cause a single foe within 30 feet to become shaken while you rage.

name: Iron Will
description: +2 bonus on Will saves.
benefits: You get a +2 bonus on all Will saving throws.

name: Ironskin Chant

prerequisites.description: Bardic music, Concentration 12 ranks, Perform 12 ranks

benefits: You can use your bardic music to grant yourself damage reduction 5/—. This effect lasts as long as you maintain the bardic music.

name: Jack of All Trades
benefits: You can use any skill, even skills that normally require training. You cannot, however, gain ranks in a skill unless you are allowed to select it.

name: Jack of All Trades (Libris Mortis)
description: You can use any skill untrained.

name: Karmic Strike
benefits: Take -4 penalty to AC to make an attack of opportunity against any melee opponent that hits you.

name: Keeper of Forbidden Lore
description: A shred of demonic racial memory grants you knowledge of numerous ancient magical secrets. You gain a bonus on Knowledge (the planes), Spellcraft, and bardic knowledge checks. The magnitude of this bonus equals the number of Abyssal heritor feats you possess. In addition, Knowledge (the planes) and Spellcraft are always class skills for you, and you can use them untrained.
source: Fiendish Codex I: Hordes of the Abyss

name: Kiai Shout
benefits: Make opponents within 30 feet shaken for 1d6 rounds (Will save). Three times per day.

name: Killoren Ancient
benefits: You gain a +4 insight bonus on Knowledge checks.

name: Killoren Destroyer
benefits: When you use your killoren smite attack ability and successfully hit a creature, that creature must make a Will save (DC 10 + 1/2 your character level + your Wis modifier) or be dazed for 1 round.

name: Killoren Hunter
benefits: You can pinpoint the location of living creatures within 30 feet as a standard action. This ability functions like blindsense.

name: Knowledge Devotion
benefits: Upon selecting this feat, you immediately add one Knowledge skill of your choice to your list of class skills. Whenever you fight a creature, you can make a Knowledge check based on its type. You then receive an insight bonus on attack rolls and damage rolls against that creature type for the remainder of the combat. The amount of the bonus depends on your Knowledge check result: DC 15 or below (+1), DC 16-25 (+2), DC 26-30 (+3), DC 31-35 (+4), DC 36 or higher (+5). You can make only one Knowledge check per creature type per combat. This benefit is an extraordinary ability.

name: Kobold Ambusher
benefits: You gain a +2 bonus on initiative checks and a +2 bonus on melee attack rolls against flat-footed opponents.

name: Landwalker
description: You can survive out of the water for 3 hours per point of Constitution. After this point you must make a Fortitude saving throw each hour (DC 15, +1 for each previous check) or begin to suffocate.

name: Law Devotion
benefits: Once per day as a swift action, you can summon the power of law to aid you. Upon activating this ability, you immediately gain a +3 sacred (if your deity is good or neutral) or profane (if your deity is evil) bonus on your attack rolls or to your AC until your next action. At the beginning of your next action, you can reallocate the bonus if desired. This effect lasts for 1 minute. The bonus increases to +5 when you attain 10th level, and to +7 when you attain 15th level.
special: You can select this feat multiple times, gaining one additional daily use each time you take it.
If you have the ability to turn or rebuke undead, you gain one additional daily use of this feat for each three daily turn or rebuke uses you expend.

name: Leap Attack
benefits: When you make a jump as part of a charge, you can double the extra damage from Power Attack.

name: Legendary Climber
benefits: You can climb up to your full speed without taking a -5 penalty on your Climb check. You do not lose your Dex bonus to AC while climbing.

name: Legendary Commander

prerequisites.description: Must rule own kingdom and have a stronghold

benefits: Your troops' morale increases by +4, you attract a legendary army, and your Leadership score is treated as 4 higher for attracting followers.

name: Legendary Rider
benefits: You can make a Ride check (DC 20) to negate any hits on your mount in combat. You do not take a penalty on Ride checks while riding bareback.

name: Legendary Tracker
benefits: You can track creatures across water, under water, or through any terrain. The DC to track increases by 20 in such conditions.

name: Legendary Wrestler
benefits: You gain a +10 bonus on all grapple checks. This stacks with bonuses from Improved Grapple and other feats.

name: Lightfeet
benefits: You can move at full speed without penalty on Balance or Move Silently checks, and you leave behind few traces when moving through natural terrain.

name: Lightning Reflexes
description: +2 bonus on Reflex saves.
benefits: You get a +2 bonus on all Reflex saving throws.

name: Lingering Damage
benefits: Any time you deal sneak attack damage, that damage persists for 1 additional round.

name: Lingering Song
benefits: The effects of your bardic music continue for 1 round after you stop performing.

name: Lord of the Uttercold

prerequisites.description: Knowledge (the planes) 9 ranks, Energy Substitution (cold), ability to cast a spell with the cold descriptor

benefits: You can turn spells with the cold descriptor into uttercold spells. Half the damage dealt by an uttercold spell is cold damage, and the other half is negative energy damage. An uttercold spell uses a spell slot of the spell's normal level.

name: Luck Devotion
benefits: Once per day as a swift action, you can activate this ability to improve your combat prowess. For 1 minute, if the result of any damage roll you make is below average, you can increase it to one-half the maximum possible (rounded up). This effect works for all damage you deal, whether from physical or magical attacks.
special: You can select this feat multiple times, gaining one additional daily use each time you take it.
If you have the ability to turn or rebuke undead, you gain one additional daily use of this feat for each three daily turn or rebuke uses you expend.

name: Lucky Break
benefits: You can expend one luck reroll as a swift action to reroll a Strength check made to break an item or burst open a door. You gain one luck reroll per day.

name: Lucky Catch
benefits: You can expend one luck reroll as an immediate action to reroll a Disable Device, Open Lock, or Sleight of Hand check. You gain one luck reroll per day.

name: Lucky Fingers
benefits: You can expend one luck reroll as an immediate action to reroll a Balance, Climb, or Jump check. You gain one luck reroll per day.

name: Lucky Start
benefits: You can expend one luck reroll to reroll an initiative check. You gain one luck reroll per day. Unlike most other luck feats, using Lucky Start requires no action.

name: Lyric Spell

prerequisites.description: Bardic music, arcane spellcaster level 6th, Perform 9 ranks

benefits: You can sacrifice uses of your bardic music to cast extra spells. As a swift action, you can spend one daily use of your bardic music to cast a spell without using a spell slot. The spell must be at least 3 levels lower than the highest level spell you can cast.

name: Mage Slayer
benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on Will saving throws. Spellcasters you threaten may not cast defensively (they automatically fail their Concentration checks to do so). Special: Taking this feat reduces your caster level for all your spells and spell-like abilities by 4.

name: Magic Devotion
benefits: Once per day as a standard action, you can launch an energy bolt as a ranged touch attack against a target within 30 feet, + 5 feet per two character levels you possess. If this attack hits, it deals 1d6 points of damage per two character levels you possess.
special: You can select this feat multiple times, gaining one additional daily use each time you take it.
If you have the ability to turn or rebuke undead, you gain one additional daily use of this feat for each two daily turn or rebuke uses you expend.

name: Magical Fortune
benefits: You can expend one luck reroll as a swift action to reroll the damage dealt by a spell you have just cast. You can expend two luck rerolls as a swift action to reroll a caster level check. You gain one luck reroll per day.

name: Make Your Own Luck
benefits: You can expend one luck reroll as an immediate action to reroll a skill check, as long as you have at least 1 rank in that skill. You gain one luck reroll per day.

name: Mark of Avernus
description: Once/encounter, make melee or ranged attack, or use a spell-like ability as an immediate action
prerequisites: Brand of the Nine Hells, allegiance to Bel
source: Fiendish Codex II: Tyrants of the Nine Hells

name: Mark of Cania
description: When you fail at an attack, gain cumulative bonus on attacks, saves, checks until you succeed
prerequisites: Brand of the Nine Hells, allegiance to Mephistopheles
source: Fiendish Codex II: Tyrants of the Nine Hells

name: Mark of Dis
description: Your starting square is not threatened, +4 to AC or on a save as immediate action 1/round
prerequisites: Brand of the Nine Hells, allegiance to Dispater
source: Fiendish Codex II: Tyrants of the Nine Hells

name: Mark of Maladomini
description: Issue pool of filth that forces Balance checks and causes creatures to become sickened
prerequisites: Brand of the Nine Hells, allegiance to Baalzebul
source: Fiendish Codex II: Tyrants of the Nine Hells

name: Mark of Malbolge
description: Gain fleshy tumors that can give you a variety of benefits
prerequisites: Brand of the Nine Hells, allegiance to Glasya
source: Fiendish Codex II: Tyrants of the Nine Hells

name: Mark of Minauros
description: Gain +2 bonus on charge attacks, and your starting square is not threatened
prerequisites: Brand of the Nine Hells, allegiance to Mammon
source: Fiendish Codex II: Tyrants of the Nine Hells

name: Mark of Nessus
description: An enemy must make a Will save before it can attack you, or it must target another foe
prerequisites: Brand of the Nine Hells, allegiance to Asmodeus
source: Fiendish Codex II: Tyrants of the Nine Hells

name: Mark of Phlegethos
description: When you take damage, you can gain bonuses on attacks, saves, or checks
prerequisites: Brand of the Nine Hells, allegiance to Belial or Fierna
source: Fiendish Codex II: Tyrants of the Nine Hells

name: Mark of Stygia
description: Do not need to make Balance checks on slippery surfaces, deal +1d6 cold damage, other benefits
prerequisites: Brand of the Nine Hells, allegiance to Levistus
source: Fiendish Codex II: Tyrants of the Nine Hells

name: Markings of the Blessed
benefits: Once per day, when you make a saving throw, you can roll twice and take the higher of the two results.

name: Markings of the Hunter
benefits: Once per day, when you make an initiative check, you can roll twice and take the higher of the two results.

name: Markings of the Magi
benefits: Once per day, when you make a caster level check, you can roll twice and take the higher of the two results.

name: Markings of the Maker
benefits: Once per day, when you make a skill check, you can roll twice and take the higher of the two results.

name: Markings of the Warrior
benefits: Once per day, when you make an attack roll, you can roll twice and take the higher of the two results.

name: Martial Stalker
benefits: Your fighter and ninja levels stack for the purpose of determining the size of your ki pool, as well as your AC bonus. Your fighter and ninja levels also stack for the purpose of qualifying for feats that require a minimum fighter level, such as Greater Weapon Focus.

name: Martial Stance
description: Learn one martial stance

name: Martial Study
description: Learn one martial maneuver (up to 3 times)

name: Martial Weapon Proficiency
description: Proficiency with all martial weapons.
benefits: You make attack rolls with martial weapons normally.

name: Master Spellthief
benefits: Your spellthief levels stack with levels of other arcane spellcaster classes for the purpose of determining what level of spell you can steal. Your spellthief and arcane spellcaster levels also stack when determining your caster level for all arcane spells. In addition, you do not incur a chance of arcane spell failure for arcane spells cast or stolen from other classes, but only if you are wearing light armor.

name: Maximize Power
benefits: When you expend your psionic focus, all variable numeric effects of a power are maximized. Using this feat increases the power point cost by 4.

name: Maximize Spell
description: Maximize spell variables.
benefits: All variable, numeric effects of a maximized spell are maximized. Maximizing a spell uses a spell slot three levels higher.

name: Maximize Spell-Like Ability

prerequisites.description: Spell-like ability at caster level 6th or higher

benefits: Choose one of your spell-like abilities to use at maximum effectiveness up to three times per day (or the ability's normal use limit, whichever is less). All variable, numeric effects of the spell-like ability are maximized. Special: This feat can be taken multiple times.

name: Mental Leap
benefits: When you expend your psionic focus, you gain a +10 bonus on a Jump check.

name: Mental Resistance
benefits: Against psionic attacks that don't allow saves, you gain a Will save to negate or reduce the effect. Against psionic attacks that allow a Fortitude or Reflex save, you can make a Will save instead. Only applies to psionic effects.
special: Cannot take this feat if you have the ability to use powers.

name: Merciful Strike
benefits: Your successful sneak attack deals nonlethal damage. When using this feat, you can ignore the usual –4 penalty on attack rolls for attempting to deal nonlethal damage with a lethal weapon. Using this feat reduces your sneak attack damage by 1d6.

name: Metamorphic Transfer
benefits: When you use a power that lets you assume a new form, you can choose one supernatural ability of the creature you transform into and gain that ability while in that form.

name: Midnight Augmentation
description: You can invest essentia to reduce the cost of augmenting psionic powers.
prerequisites: Ability to manifest powers, ability to invest essentia

name: Midnight Dodge
description: You can invest essentia to increase your AC against one opponent.
prerequisites: Dex 13, Dodge, ability to invest essentia

name: Midnight Metamagic
description: You can invest essentia to reduce the spell slot increase for metamagic feats.
prerequisites: Any metamagic feat, ability to invest essentia

name: Mind Drain
benefits: Your successful sneak attack drains power points from your target equal to its manifester level (minimum 1). If this attack reduces your target to 0 power points, the opponent also loses any psionic focus. A target that has no power points when you make the sneak attack is not affected by this feat. You can't use this feat on the same target more than once per round. Using this feat reduces your sneak attack damage by 1d6.

name: Mind Over Body
benefits: At each level, you can gain 1 hit point or 1 power point. Once you make this choice, it cannot be changed.

name: Miser's Fortune
benefits: Whenever an opponent makes a sunder attack or Strength check to damage an object within 30 feet of you, you can expend one luck reroll as an immediate action to force that opponent to reroll. In addition, as long as you still have one luck reroll remaining for the day, items in your possession receive a +5 luck bonus on saving throws. You gain one luck reroll per day.

name: Mitigate Suffering
benefits: As long as you have any restoration spell or another conjuration (healing) spell that cures ability damage available to cast, you can confer temporary ability points on yourself or an ally. As a standard action, you can create 2 points + 1 point per level of the highest-level such spell you have available to cast. These ability points can be applied to any single damaged ability, raising it to a maximum of its starting score. Temporary ability points granted in this way disappear after 10 minutes. As a secondary benefit, you gain a +1 competence bonus to your caster level when casting conjuration (healing) spells.

name: Mobile Spellcasting
benefits: You can cast a spell and move at the same time. When you cast a spell, you can also take a 5-foot step before, during, or after casting.

name: Mobility
description: +4 dodge bonus to AC against attacks of opportunity.
benefits: You get a +4 dodge bonus to Armor Class against attacks of opportunity caused when you move out of or within a threatened area.

name: Monkey Grip
benefits: Use melee weapons one size category larger than you with a -2 penalty, but the effort required doesn't change.

name: Moradin's Smile
benefits: You gain a +2 bonus on all Charisma-based skill checks.

name: Multispell
benefits: You may cast one additional quickened spell per round.

name: Multiweapon Fighting
benefits: Penalties for fighting with multiple weapons are reduced by 2.

name: Music of the Outer Spheres
description: You can use your bardic music to create discordant, insane sounds particularly effective against aberrations. You gain three additional bardic music abilities: Lull Aberration (slows aberrations), Sicken Aberration (makes aberrations sickened), and Vitalize Aberration (+2 morale bonus to special attack DCs and +1 dodge bonus to AC for aberrations).

name: Narrow Mind
benefits: +4 bonus on Concentration checks made to become psionically focused.

name: Natural Bond
benefits: Add +3 to your effective druid level when determining your animal companion's abilities.

name: Natural Trickster
benefits: Choose one 1st-level illusion spell. You can use that spell as a spell-like ability once per day (caster level equals your character level).

name: Necromantic Might
description: Your necromancy spells are more difficult to resist.

name: Necromantic Presence
description: Your presence unnerves living creatures and empowers undead.
prerequisites: Ability to rebuke or command undead, Charisma 13+

name: Necropolis Born
benefits: An innate talent for magic grants you the following spell-like abilities as a 1st-level caster: 1/day—cause fear, ghost sound, touch of fatigue.

name: Negative Energy Burst
benefits: You can use one rebuke undead attempt to unleash a wave of negative energy in a 60-foot burst, dealing 1d6 damage per character level to living creatures.

name: Night Haunt
benefits: An innate talent for magic grants you the following spell-like abilities as a 1st-level caster: 1/day—dancing lights, prestidigitation, unseen servant.

name: Nimble Bones
description: Skeletons you create are faster and more agile than normal.
prerequisites: Corpsecrafter, ability to cast animate dead

name: Nonlethal Substitution

prerequisites.description: Knowledge (arcana) 5 ranks, any other metamagic feat

benefits: Choose one type of energy (acid, cold, electricity, or fire). You can then modify any spell with the chosen descriptor to deal nonlethal damage instead of normal energy damage. A nonlethal spell uses a spell slot one level higher than the spell's normal level.

name: Obscure Lore
benefits: You gain a +4 bonus on bardic knowledge or lore checks.

name: Obtain Familiar
benefits: You can obtain a familiar in the same manner as a sorcerer or wizard. Obtaining a familiar takes 24 hours and uses up magic materials worth 100 gp.

name: Ocular Spell
description: You can cast a spell as an ocular spell. An ocular spell does not take effect immediately, but is instead held in one of your eyes for up to 8 hours. You can store only two ocular spells. When released, they become brilliant blasts that shoot out from your eyes as rays with a range of up to 60 feet. An ocular spell uses up a spell slot two levels higher than the spell's actual level.

name: Old Salt
description: You gain a +1 bonus on Balance, Profession (sailor), and Use Rope checks. You can use a Profession (sailor) check to predict the weather (as described in the Survival skill).

name: Open Minded
benefits: You immediately gain 5 skill points. Spend these skill points as normal. If you spend them on a cross-class skill, you gain ranks as if the skill points were spent on a class skill (1 skill point = 1 rank).

name: Open Greater Chakra
description: You can bind one soulmeld to your throat or waist chakra.
prerequisites: Ability to shape soulmelds, Open Lesser Chakra

name: Open Least Chakra
description: You can bind one soulmeld to your crown, feet, or hands chakra.

name: Open Lesser Chakra
description: You can bind one soulmeld to your arms, brow, or shoulders chakra.
prerequisites: Ability to shape soulmelds, Open Least Chakra

name: Opportunity Power
benefits: When you expend your psionic focus, you can make attacks of opportunity with touch powers. Using this feat increases the power point cost by 6.

name: Ordered Chaos
description: You are an unusually lawful Abyssal heritor. Your alignment is not affected by the Abyssal heritor feats you possess. Spells and effects that are keyed to alignment affect you as if you were chaotic, as well as your actual alignment.
source: Fiendish Codex I: Hordes of the Abyss

name: Otherworldly Countenance
description: You are either stunningly beautiful or wretchedly hideous. Either way, your appearance can be terribly unsettling to others upon whom you focus your attentions. You gain a bonus on Perform checks (if you chose beauty) or Intimidate checks (if you chose ugliness) equal to the number of Abyssal heritor feats you possess. As a full-round action, you can attempt to fascinate (if beautiful) or sicken (if ugly) a target within 30 feet a number of times per day equal to the number of Abyssal heritor feats you possess.
source: Fiendish Codex I: Hordes of the Abyss

name: Overchannel
benefits: When manifesting a power, you can increase your effective manifester level by 1, but take 1d8 points of damage. For each additional +1 to manifester level, you take an additional 3d8 points of damage (max +5).

name: Oversized Two-Weapon Fighting
benefits: You can treat a one-handed weapon in your off hand as if it were a light weapon for the purpose of two-weapon fighting penalties.

name: Overwhelming Critical
benefits: When using the chosen weapon, you deal +1d6 points of bonus damage on a successful critical hit.

name: Pain Touch

prerequisites.description: Wis 15, Stunning Fist, base attack bonus +2

benefits: Victims of your stunning attack become nauseated for 1 round after being stunned.

name: Parrying Shield
description: You have studied advanced techniques for battling foes whose attacks normally bypass armor. You use your shield to block or deflect rays and other touch attacks. You gain your shield bonus (and the shield's enhancement bonus, if any) as an addition to your touch AC.

name: Perfect Clarity of Mind and Body
description: Advanced Diamond Mind tactics

name: Perfect Health
benefits: You are immune to all nonmagical diseases, as well as magical diseases of your choice (up to your Con bonus).

name: Perfect Two-Weapon Fighting
benefits: You can make any number of attacks with your off-hand weapon in a full attack action.

name: Permanent Emanation
benefits: You can make one spell with a range of personal and a duration measured in rounds permanent. The spell is always active.

name: Persistent Attacker
benefits: If your sneak attack hits, your first attack against that creature on your next turn is also considered a sneak attack even if it wouldn't normally qualify. Using this feat reduces your first sneak attack's damage by 4d6. The resulting second sneak attack deals its full extra damage.

name: Persistent Spell
benefits: A persistent spell with a fixed or personal range lasts for 24 hours. The spell must have a duration of at least 1 round and must affect one or more creatures or objects (not an area). A persistent spell uses up a spell slot six levels higher than the spell's actual level.

name: Persistent Refusal
description: Make another save against ongoing effect
prerequisites: Ability to turn or rebuke undead
source: Fiendish Codex II: Tyrants of the Nine Hells

name: Phalanx Fighting

prerequisites.description: Proficiency with a heavy shield, base attack bonus +1

benefits: With heavy shield and light weapon, gain +1 AC. Adjacent allies with this feat form shield wall for +2 AC and +1 Reflex saves.

name: Pierce Magical Concealment
benefits: You can ignore spell-based concealment of creatures you attack.

name: Pierce Magical Protection
benefits: You can ignore spell-based bonuses to Armor Class.

name: Piercing Sight
benefits: You gain a +4 bonus on saving throws to disbelieve illusions.

name: Pin Shield
benefits: Give up all off-hand attacks to pin opponent's shield, removing its AC bonus until end of your action.

name: Pious Defiance
description: Gain bonus on Will saves equal to 1/2 character level as an immediate action
prerequisites: Ability to turn or rebuke undead
source: Fiendish Codex II: Tyrants of the Nine Hells

name: Planar Turning
benefits: You can use a turn/rebuke undead attempt to turn or rebuke outsiders as if they were undead.

name: Plant Devotion
benefits: Once per day as an immediate action, you can force your body to take on plant characteristics. This effect lasts for 1 minute. While in this form, your natural armor bonus increases by 2. In addition, you gain light fortification (25% chance to ignore extra damage from critical hits and sneak attacks). The resistance chance granted by this fortification effect increases to 50% when you attain 10th level and to 75% when you attain 15th level. At 20th level, you gain heavy fortification (immune to critical hits and sneak attack damage) while in this form.
special: You can select this feat multiple times, gaining one additional daily use each time you take it.
If you have the ability to turn or rebuke undead, you gain one additional daily use of this feat for each two daily turn or rebuke uses you expend.

name: Point Blank Shot
description: +1 bonus on ranged attack and damage rolls within 30 feet.
benefits: You get a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls with ranged weapons at ranges of up to 30 feet.

name: Poison Expert
benefits: Choose a type of poison (contact, ingested, inhaled, or injury). The DC to resist both the initial and secondary damage of poisons of this type that you create and use increases by 1. You can gain this feat multiple times. Its effect does not stack. Each time you take the feat, it applies to a new type of poison.

name: Poison Master
benefits: Choose a type of poison (contact, ingested, inhaled, or injury) for which you have selected the Poison Expert feat. The initial and secondary damage dealt by poisons of this type that you create and use increases by 1 point per die of damage (or by 1 point, if it deals a fixed amount of damage). You can gain this feat multiple times. Its effect does not stack. Each time you take the feat, it applies to a new type of poison for which you have selected Poison Expert.

name: Poison Healer
description: Poison isn't always bad for you. Any time you succeed on a Fortitude save against a poison, you heal a number of hit points of damage equal to your Constitution bonus.
source: Fiendish Codex I: Hordes of the Abyss

name: Poison Talons
description: Your claws drip with poison. Once per hour, you can secrete poison onto your claws as a swift action. This poison remains in place until you damage a target with your claws. At that point, the creature struck must succeed on a Fortitude save (DC 10 + 1/2 your character level + your Con modifier) or take 1d4 points of Strength damage. One minute later, the victim must save again or take an additional 1d4 points of Strength damage.
prerequisites: Claws of the Beast, any two other Abyssal heritor feats
source: Fiendish Codex I: Hordes of the Abyss

name: Polyglot
benefits: You can speak all languages. If the creature is using telepathy, you are affected by that telepathy as if you shared a common language.

name: Positive Energy Aura
benefits: You emanate a 20-foot aura of positive energy. You can expend one turn undead attempt to activate the aura for 1 minute.

name: Positive Energy Resistance
description: You have some resistance to positive energy effects.

name: Power Attack
description: Trade attack bonus for damage on melee attacks.
benefits: You can subtract a number from all melee attack rolls and add the same number to all melee damage rolls.

name: Power Critical

prerequisites.description: Weapon Focus with weapon, base attack bonus +4

benefits: Gain +4 bonus on rolls to confirm critical threats with selected weapon. Special: Can be taken multiple times, effects stack if same weapon.

name: Power Penetration
benefits: +4 bonus on manifester level checks to overcome power resistance.

name: Power Specialization
benefits: When you expend your psionic focus, add +2 to the damage dealt by a power that deals hit point damage.

name: Power Throw
benefits: You can use Power Attack with thrown weapons. Add your Strength bonus (not your Dexterity bonus) to damage rolls with thrown weapons.

name: Powerful Wild Shape
benefits: You retain your powerful build trait when using wild shape, allowing you to function as one size category larger for purposes of grappling, bull rushes, etc.

name: Practiced Spellcaster
benefits: Choose a spellcasting class that you possess. Your caster level for that class increases by +4. This benefit can't increase your caster level to higher than your Hit Dice. Special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Each time you take the feat, it applies to a different spellcasting class.

name: Precise Shot
description: No penalty for shooting into melee.
benefits: You can shoot or throw ranged weapons at an opponent engaged in melee without taking the standard -4 penalty on your attack roll.

name: Precognitive Visions
description: You periodically experience visions from the near future. Your visions reflect possible futures, and you can act to ensure or resist them. You gain a number of floating +1 insight bonuses equal to one-half the number of Abyssal heritor feats you possess (minimum 1). The bonuses last 24 hours, and you must reassign them at the start of each new day. You can assign each bonus to your AC, attack rolls, initiative checks, one saving throw, or checks made with one skill.
prerequisites: Wis 13, any one Abyssal heritor feat
source: Fiendish Codex I: Hordes of the Abyss

name: Primordial Scion
description: The Abyss beckons. Your natural weapons, as well as any weapons you wield, are treated as chaotic-aligned for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction. In addition, attacks you make with such weapons deal an extra 1d6 points of damage to lawful creatures. This bonus increases by an additional 1d6 for every four Abyssal heritor feats you possess.
prerequisites: Chaotic alignment, any one Abyssal heritor feat
source: Fiendish Codex I: Hordes of the Abyss

name: Prone Attack

prerequisites.description: Dex 15, Lightning Reflexes, base attack bonus +2

benefits: Attack from prone without penalty and stand as free action if you hit. Opponents gain no bonus against you while prone.

name: Protection Devotion
benefits: Once per day as an immediate action, you can activate a protective aura. While it is active, you gain a +2 sacred (if your deity is good or neutral) or profane (if your deity is evil) bonus to AC, as does every ally within 30 feet of you. This bonus increases by 1 for every four character levels you possess (maximum +7 at 20th level). This effect lasts for 1 minute.
special: You can select this feat multiple times, gaining one additional daily use each time you take it.
If you have the ability to turn or rebuke undead, you gain one additional daily use of this feat for each three daily turn or rebuke uses you expend.

name: Protective Ward
benefits: As long as you have an abjuration spell available to cast, you can use a standard action to provide a sacred (if your deity is good or neutral) or profane (if your deity is evil) bonus to AC equal to the level of the highest-level abjuration spell you have available to cast. You can apply this bonus either to your AC or to that of a single ally within 30 feet, and it persists until the beginning of your next turn. As a secondary benefit, you gain a +1 competence bonus to your caster level when casting abjuration spells.

name: Psicrystal Affinity
benefits: You gain a psicrystal, a fragment of your personality in crystalline form.

name: Psicrystal Containment
benefits: Your psicrystal can hold your psionic focus. When you take an action that would normally expend your focus, your psicrystal can expend its stored focus instead (if within 5 feet).

name: Psionic Affinity
benefits: +2 bonus on Psicraft and Use Psionic Device checks.

name: Psionic Body
benefits: Gain +2 hit points for each psionic feat you possess (including this one).

name: Psionic Charge
benefits: While you are psionically focused, you can make a charge while following an erratic path. Opponents don't get the usual +2 bonus to attack rolls when you charge.

name: Psionic Dodge
benefits: While you are psionically focused, you gain a +1 dodge bonus to AC.

name: Psionic Endowment
benefits: When you expend your psionic focus, add +1 to the save DC of a power you manifest.

name: Psionic Fist
benefits: When you expend your psionic focus, your unarmed strike or natural weapon attack deals an extra 2d6 points of damage.

name: Psionic Hole
benefits: You are anathema to psionic creatures and characters. Psionic foes within 30 feet of you lose their psionic focus and lose 1 power point per round.
special: Cannot take this feat if you have the ability to use powers.

name: Psionic Meditation
benefits: You can take a move action to become psionically focused (normally a full-round action).

name: Psionic Shot
benefits: When you expend your psionic focus, your ranged weapon attack deals an extra 2d6 points of damage.

name: Psionic Talent
benefits: Gain 2 additional power points.

name: Psionic Weapon
benefits: When you expend your psionic focus, your melee weapon attack deals an extra 2d6 points of damage.

name: Psithief
benefits: You can use your steal spell ability to siphon psionic energy instead of spell energy. Instead of stealing a spell, you can choose to steal a number of power points equal twice to the maximum level of spell you can steal minus 1 (up to a maximum value equal to the manifester level of the creature struck). You can use the stolen power points only to manifest a psionic power you already know. You must use these power points within 1 hour of stealing them. In addition, Knowledge (psionics) and Psicraft are spellthief class skills for you.

name: Psycarnum Blade
description: You can invest essentia to enhance your mind blade.
prerequisites: Mind blade class feature, ability to invest essentia

name: Psycarnum Crystal
description: You can invest essentia to enhance your psicrystal.

name: Psycarnum Infusion
description: You can invest essentia to increase the save DC of your psionic powers.
prerequisites: Ability to manifest powers, ability to invest essentia

name: Psychic Luck
benefits: You can expend one luck reroll as a swift action to reroll the damage dealt by a psionic power you have just manifested. You can expend two luck rerolls as a swift action to reroll a manifester level check. You gain one luck reroll per day.

name: Quick Reconnoiter
benefits: You can make a Spot check and a Listen check as free actions. You also gain a +2 bonus on initiative checks.

name: Quick Recovery
description: Whenever you begin your turn stunned or dazed, you can make a new saving throw at the original DC of the effect in an attempt to recover. Recovering is a move action. If the effect did not allow a saving throw, you can recover by succeeding on a Will save (DC 10 + 1/2 HD or caster level of originator + relevant ability modifier).

name: Quicken Power
benefits: When you expend your psionic focus, you can manifest a power as a free action. Using this feat increases the power point cost by 6.

name: Quicken Turning
description: You can attempt to turn or rebuke undead as a free action.
prerequisites: Ability to turn or rebuke undead

name: Ranged Disarm

prerequisites.description: Dex 15, Point Blank Shot, Precise Shot, base attack bonus +5

benefits: Disarm a foe from up to 30 feet away with a ranged weapon.

name: Ranged Inspiration
benefits: Double the range of your bardic music abilities.

name: Ranged Pin

prerequisites.description: Dex 15, Point Blank Shot, Precise Shot, base attack bonus +5

benefits: Use a ranged weapon to grapple a foe within 30 feet.

name: Ranged Spell Specialization
benefits: You get a +2 bonus on damage rolls with ranged spells.

name: Ranged Sunder

prerequisites.description: Str 13, Point Blank Shot, Precise Shot, Ranged Pin, base attack bonus +5

benefits: Use a ranged weapon to sunder a foe's weapon at reduced damage penalty.

name: Rapid Inspiration
benefits: You can activate your bardic music as a swift action instead of a standard action.

name: Rapid Metabolism
benefits: You heal hit points and ability damage at twice the normal rate.

name: Rapid Shot
description: Make one extra ranged attack per round at -2 penalty.
benefits: You can get one extra attack per round with a ranged weapon. The attack is at your highest base attack bonus, but each attack you make in that round takes a -2 penalty.

name: Rapid Stunning

prerequisites.description: Combat Reflexes, Stunning Fist, base attack bonus +6

benefits: Use one additional stunning attack per round.

name: Rapid Regeneration
description: Regeneration increases by 1
source: Fiendish Codex II: Tyrants of the Nine Hells

name: Rapid Swimming
description: Your swim speed increases by 20 feet.
prerequisites: Natural swim speed, base Fortitude save +2

name: Razing Strike

prerequisites.description: Ability to cast 3rd-level arcane or divine spells, sneak attack

benefits: As a swift action, you can spend a spell slot to gain a bonus on attack and damage rolls against constructs or undead for 1 round. The bonus equals the level of the spell slot spent.

name: Reaping Talons
description: Advanced Tiger Claw two-weapon tactics

name: Reckless Offense
benefits: When you use the full attack action, you can take a –4 penalty to AC to gain a +2 bonus on melee attack rolls.

name: Reckless Wand Wielder

prerequisites.description: Craft Wand, Use Magic Device 1 rank

benefits: When using a wand, you can increase the wand's caster level by expending an additional charge from the wand.

name: Reflect Arrows
benefits: When you deflect an arrow or other ranged attack, it reflects back at the attacker, using the original attack roll.

name: Repeat Spell
benefits: A repeated spell is automatically cast again at the beginning of your next round of actions. No changes can be made to the spell. A repeated spell uses up a spell slot three levels higher than the spell's actual level.

name: Retrieve Spell

prerequisites.description: Any two divine feats, ability to turn or rebuke undead

benefits: As a standard action, you can expend turn or rebuke undead attempts to recover a spell you have already cast that day. You must spend a number of daily uses equal to 1 + the level of the spell you wish to recover. You can recover only spells within the class that grants your turn or rebuke undead ability.

name: Return Shot
benefits: When you expend your psionic focus, if you are the target of a ranged attack, you can deflect the missile back at the attacker, making a ranged attack (with all normal modifiers).

name: Righteous Strike
benefits: Your unarmed strikes are treated as lawful-aligned weapons, dealing +2d6 damage against chaotic creatures.

name: Roundabout Kick

prerequisites.description: Str 15, Improved Unarmed Strike, Power Attack

benefits: Make an additional unarmed attack against an opponent on which you just scored a critical hit.

name: Ruinous Rage
benefits: While in a rage, you gain a +8 bonus to Strength (instead of the normal +4).

name: Sacred Vengeance
benefits: Spend a turn/rebuke attempt to add 2d6 damage to all melee attacks for 1 round.

name: Sahuagin Flip
description: After making a single melee attack, you can use the withdraw action (moving up to twice your swim speed) as a move action. You can only use this feat while swimming.

name: Sailor's Balance
description: You gain a +5 competence bonus on Balance checks made while on the deck or in the rigging of a ship. You can move across a slippery deck at your normal speed.

name: Sanctify Water
description: By expending a daily turn undead attempt, you can infuse the water around you with positive energy. All creatures in a 20-foot radius take damage as though struck by holy water. The water retains positive energy for 1 + Charisma modifier rounds.
prerequisites: Cha 13, ability to channel positive energy

name: Sanctum Spell
benefits: Choose a specific location as your sanctum. A sanctum spell uses up a spell slot one level lower than usual when cast within your sanctum (minimum 0-level), and one level higher than usual when cast outside your sanctum.

name: BLESSED BY TEM-ET-NU
description: Tem-Et-Nu has marked you as having an important destiny in her temple. defeated a hippopotamus in single combat. Benefit: Hippopotami cannot attack you unless they are magically compelled. In addition, if you possess the turn or rebuke undead class feature, you can rebuke and command hippopotami as an evil cleric rebukes or commands undead. You gain a +2 sacred bonus to your Armor Class against chaotic-aligned creatures with the fire subtype. Cleric is now an additional favored class for you; the cleric class does not count when determining whether you take an experience point penalty for multiclassing. Special: Your aura radiates law with a strength equal to your character level (see detect law, page 219 of the Player’s Handbook). If you ever lose favor with Tem-Et-Nu, or change your patron deity to another deity, you lose all benefits of this feat and take damage as if you were bitten by a hippopotamus. You do not gain a replacement feat. If you later return to Tem-Et-Nu’s faithful and receive an atonement spell, you regain the benefit of this feat. Benefit: As long as you are under the effects of a rage, you gain the fire subtype. You gain immunity to fire, but have vulnerability to cold, which means you take +50% damage from cold, regardless of whether a saving throw is allowed, or if the save is a success or failure. Sandskimmer: At 7th level, a wasteland ranger gains Sandskimmer as a bonus feat. This benefit replaces the standard ranger’s woodland stride class feature. Waste Hunter (Ex): At 10th level, a wasteland ranger chooses a specific kind of favored enemy native to the waste from among the types or subtypes he has already chosen. (For example, he could choose dunewinders if he has magical beasts as a favored enemy.) He gains an additional +2 bonus on weapon damage rolls, and on Bluff, Listen, Sense Motive, Spot, and Survival checks against creatures of this kind. In addition, the ranger gains a +2 bonus on saves against the chosen creature’s special abilities and damage reduction 2/– against its physical attacks. This benefit replaces the new favored enemy gained at 10th level by a standard ranger, but the waste ranger’s bonuses against previously selected favored enemies increase as if he had gained a new favored enemy.

name: LIGHT OF AURIFAR
description: Your unarmed strikes echo with thunder, stunning your foe and those nearby. Stunning Fist. Benefit: You must declare that you are using this feat before you make your attack roll (thus, a failed attack roll ruins the attempt). By expending one Stunning Fist use for the day, you force an opponent successfully struck by your unarmed attack, and all creatures adjacent to that opponent, to make a Fortitude saving throw (DC 10 + 1/2 your character level + your Wis modifier) or be stunned for 1 round. The opponent struck by the unarmed attack is dealt damage normally. This feat can be used only once per round. Special: A fighter may select Pharaoh’s Fist as one of his fighter bonus feats.

name: PRIEST OF THE WASTE
description: You can swap out prepared spells for others that aid in exploring and surviving in wastelands. or Water domain. Benefit: You can use a spell slot currently occupied by a prepared spell that is not a domain spell to cast a spell Having observed the ways of a desert viper, you have learned to use ki in a fashion similar to poison. Unarmed Strike, Stunning Fist. Benefit: You must declare that you are using this feat before you make your attack roll (thus, a failed attack roll ruins the attempt). By expending one of your Stunning Fist uses for the day, you imbue your next unarmed attack with disruptive ki that mimics the effects of mild viper venom. If that attack hits, it deals normal damage and forces the target to make a Fortitude saving throw (DC 10 + 1/2 your character level + your Wis modifier). A creature that fails the saving throw takes 1d3 points of Constitution damage and must make a similar saving throw 1 minute later or take another 1d3 points of Constitution damage. This feat can be used only once per round. Special: A fighter may select Rattlesnake Strike as one of his fighter bonus feats.

name: RATTLESNAKE STRIKE
description: Undead that you turn or rebuke immolate. to either the Fire or Sun domain. Benefit: Any undead that you successfully turn or rebuke take 2d6 points of fire damage in addition to the normal turning or rebuking effect. of equal or lower level that appears on the following list: cloak of shade†, control weather, create food and water, create water, endure elements, heroes’ feast, hydrate†, protection from dessication†, protection from energy, and resist energy. † New domain or spell described in Chapter 5.
prerequisites: Ability to turn or rebuke undead, access

name: SAND CAMOUFLAGE
description: You can hide yourself in sand with a moment’s notice. Benefit: As a standard action, you can quickly camouflage yourself in any area of ash, dust, loose earth, or sand that is at least 1 inch deep. In areas with 1 to 11 inches of appropriate loose soil, you gain a +4 bonus on Hide checks. In areas with 12 inches or more of appropriate loose soil, you instead gain a +10 bonus on Hide checks. You must still have cover or concealment to make a Hide check, as described on page 76 of the Player’s Handbook. pqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqrs

name: DETERMINING LOOSE SOIL
description: Many of the feats in this section rely on knowing the composition of surrounding soil and work to best advantage only if the ground is made up of or covered by a layer of ash, dust, loose earth, or sand. Environment Chance of 1 Inch Loose Soil* Desert 100% Desert ruin/dungeon 100% Desert dwelling/city 90% Forest 80% Forest ruin/dungeon 70% Forest dwelling/city 60% Plains 70% Plains ruin/dungeon 60% Plains dwelling/city 50% Hills 60% It can be assumed that most waste environments, whether outside or within a ruin, have either a sandy surface or a requisite layer of ash, dust, loose earth, or sand 1 or more inches thick. In other environments, the following table can be used to make this determination. Environment Chance of 1 Inch Loose Soil* Hills ruin/dungeon 50% Hills city/dwelling 40% Mountains 50% Mountains ruin/dungeon 40% Mountains dwelling/city 30% Marsh 10% Marsh ruin/dungeon 10% Marsh dwelling/city 10% * Material can be ash, dust, grit, loose earth, or sand. pqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqrs

name: SAND DANCER
description: While making another attack, you attempt to blind a foe with thrown sand. Benefit: In any round when you first move at least 10 feet using your Tumble skill in an area covered in a layer of at least 1 inch of ash, dust, loose earth, or sand, you can supplement an attack made in that same round with flung or kicked material. A foe damaged by your attack must make a Fortitude saving throw (DC 10 + 1/2 your character level + your Wis modifier) or be blinded for 1 round.

name: SAND SNARE
description: When you knock your foes into the sand, they have a hard time regaining their feet. Combat Expertise, Improved Trip. Benefit: If you successfully trip an opponent in any area of ash, dust, loose earth, or sand that is at least 1 inch deep, that opponent must take a fullround action to stand during the round after being tripped. Following that round, the target can get to his feet with a move action (as normal). The opponent still provokes attacks of opportunity while attempting to stand, as normal. can take a standard action to hurl a spray of sand into the eyes of all creatures in a 5-foot-radius burst. Those within the area must make Fortitude saving throws (DC 10 + 1/2 your character level + your Wis modifier) or be blinded for 1 round.

name: SANDSKIMMER
description: You are particularly adept at moving over sand. Benefit: You treat shallow sand as normal terrain and deep sand as shallow sand. Sand crust does not break beneath your feet, allowing you to move atop it as if it were normal terrain. Special: This feat can be taken twice, allowing a creature to treat all sand as normal terrain. Its effects also stack similarly with other abilities that improve movement over sand. For example, if a bhuka takes Sandskimmer, she treats all sand as normal terrain and never breaks through sand crust by moving across it.

name: SCORPION’S
description: GRASP Like the scorpion, you can grab and hold your prey. Str 13, Improved Unarmed Strike, Improved Grapple. Benefit: If your attack with an unarmed strike or a light or one-handed melee weapon hits, the strike deals normal damage and you can attempt to start a grapple as a free action; no initial touch attack is required. If you succeed in starting the grapple, you must drop your onehanded weapon, but if you started the grapple with a light weapon, you can use Lidda blinds her foe with the Sand Dancer feat it in each round to make an attack

name: DRIFT MAGIC
description: You can tap the power of drift magic. 4 ranks, ability to cast spells. Benefit: You can use drift magic, as described on page 105.

name: SAND SPINNER
description: against the creature you are grappling without taking You spray sand with your acrobatic maneuvers. the normal –4 penalty on the attack roll. name: SCORPION’S RESOLVE
description: Like the scorpion, you are not easily distracted. Benefit: You gain a +4 bonus on saving throws against mind-affecting spells and abilities.

name: SCORPION’S SENSE
description: Like the scorpion, you sense other creatures simply by perceiving their contact with the sand. Benefit: You are sensitive to vibrations in the ground and can automatically pinpoint the location of anything that is moving and in contact with the ground so long as it is within 10 feet. The range expands to 20 feet if you are in an area covered in a layer of at least 1 inch of ash, dust, loose earth, or sand. This ability only tells you what square the creature is in, so you still have the normal miss chance from concealment if you cannot see the creature.

name: SEARING SPELL
description: Your fire spells are so hot that they can damage creatures that normally have resistance or immunity to fire. Benefit: A searing spell is so hot that it ignores the resistance to fire of creatures affected by the spell, and affected creatures with immunity to fire still take half damage. This feat can be applied only to spells with the fire descriptor.

name: SERPENT FANG
description: You are able to project your ki to strike foes as though you had extended reach. Stunning Fist. Benefit: You must declare that you are using this feat before you make your attack roll (thus, a failed attack roll ruins the attempt). By expending one Stunning Fist use for the day, you can project your ki to strike an opponent that is 5 feet beyond your normal unarmed reach. If the blow hits, it deals normal unarmed damage. This feat can be used only once per round. Special: Monks can use special monk weapons when using this feat, dealing damage according to the weapon.

name: SCORPION’S INSTINCTS
description: Creatures with the cold subtype take double damage from a searing spell. Creatures affected by a searing spell are still entitled to whatever saving throw the spell normally allows. A searing spell uses up a spell slot one level higher than the spell’s actual level. You have the option to conduct the grapple normally, or you may hold a creature one or more sizes smaller than you with your off hand. If you choose to do the latter, you take a –20 penalty on grapple checks against that creature and you cannot deal damage with your grapple checks, but you are not considered grappled yourself. You don’t lose your Dexterity bonus to AC, you still threaten an area, and you can use remaining attacks against other opponents. While maintaining this latter type of hold, you can move normally (possibly carrying your opponent away), provided you can drag the opponent’s weight.

name: TOUCHSTONE
description: You forge a link with a power-rich location, referred to as a touchstone site. area in which the touchstone lies, or possession of a touchstone key (a portable object native to the touchstone’s area and worth at least 250 gp). To forge a link between yourself and the touchstone, you must spend a day in meditation, spending 10 XP and 250 gp in material components. Alternatively, the key object for the touchstone can be destroyed as part of the ritual, in place of the material components. Benefit: You gain the base ability associated with the touchstone to which you are linked. If you physically visit the location to which you have forged a link, you can also gain a higher-order ability with a limited number of uses, as described for that touchstone. You can swap sites any time you visit a new one and fulfill its higher-order recharge condition. See Touchstone Sites for more information. Special: You can take this feat more than once. Each time you do, you are able to retain one more touchstone site’s base ability. You also gain the capacity to retain uses of one more touchstone’s higher-order ability. Thus, by taking this feat twice, you could retain the base abilities for both the Shrine at Kahar and the Sunken City of Pazar simultaneously, as well as both sites’ higher-order abilities and uses. If you then visit a third touchstone site and fulfill its recharge condition, unless you have taken this feat three times, you must choose which of your two previous touchstone site abilities to lose as you gain the abilities of the new touchstone site.

name: TOUCHSTONE SITES
description: Higher-Order Uses: Each time a character recharges her higher-order ability, she gains a number of uses of that ability. No matter what the number of total uses, a higher-order ability cannot be used more than once per day. Certain places resonate with unique forms of energy. Those who take the Touchstone feat can forge a link with these special locales, thereby gaining a special ability. Actually visiting a touchstone site can provide even more potent abilities.

name: EAGLE’S FURY
description: You know how to wield the eagle’s claw with deadly speed. Benefit: You can get one extra attack per round with an eagle’s claw (see page 96). The attack is at your highest base attack bonus, but each attack you make in that round (the extra one and the normal ones) takes a –2 penalty. You must take a full attack action to use this feat.

name: SUNKEN CITY OF PAZAR (EL 4)
description: Statue Sunken City of Pazar Subsequent Encounters: Once the characters clear the sunken city of foes, subsequent visits have a 10% chance of an encounter. If the PCs fail to visit the location for more than a year, roll for an encounter as if they had never visited the location. Base Ability: Once per day, for a period of 1 minute, you function in many ways as if you were one size larger than normal. While so functioning, you gain the bonuses associated with being one size larger for the purposes of opposed checks (such as grapple checks and bull rush attempts), but not the penalties. You are also considered one size larger for the purposes of the effects of any ability (such as improved grab or swallow whole). Further, while this ability functions, you can use weapons designed for a creature one size category larger than you without penalty. Your space and reach remain those of a creature of your actual size. The benefits of this ability do not stack with any similar ability. Recharge Condition: Rub the brass lamp and defeat the summoned djinni in a grappling contest. Higher-Order Ability: You maintain your altered size for 8 hours (or until you dismiss the effect). Higher-Order Uses: 4. The upper half of a marble statue thrusts from the ash dunes of the surrounding desert. The statue’s noble face is pockmarked and scarred by the ravages of the elements, and it holds a tablet, partially buried in sand. If uncovered, the tablet is too scoured by the sands for anyone to comprehend what might once have been inscribed upon it. The weight of those who spend any time at all in front of the statue causes a stone in the buried dome beneath to give way, dropping the visitors down onto the side of a sand pile inside the buried dome. (The sand blunts the 30-foot fall, dealing only 2d6 points of damage.) Within a small complex of ruins beneath the dome (which is half filled with sand) is a platform on which a large brass lamp is permanently affi xed. It once held a potent magic. Initial Encounter: Roll on the Sunken City of Pazar Encounters table when the characters visit the site for the first time. 76–97 Chekryan† 98–100 Djinni † New monster described on page 142.

name: (EL 5)
description: Rubble Like the shrine at Kahar, the pool found amid the ruins of an ancient temple at Abu-Ima is reputed to have the ability to heal the sick and cure the afflicted. However, unlike the shrine at Kahar, the pool lost in the ruins of Abu-Ima is not a popular destination—the curse that fell on Abu-Ima killed all its inhabitants, and now awful creatures and visions of hell are said to roam its sand-filled lanes. Initial Encounter: Roll on the Healing Waters of Abu-Ima Encounters table when the characters visit the site for the first time.
